title: Máddu spine event
description: The published contract for a single append-only spine event. Generated from template/maddu/runtime/lib/event-schema.mjs — do not edit by hand. Data fields are typed when present; the payload is open (extra keys are valid, additive-only within a MAJOR).

data.description: Máddu was installed into a repo for the first time.
An existing install was upgraded to a new framework version.
The bridge server started and bound its port.
A `maddu doctor` integrity run completed with per-check results.
A `maddu audit` governance run completed with per-check results.
An agent session was registered (explicitly or by a hook).
A live session reported it is still active.
A session ended, optionally leaving a handoff note.
A session took exclusive ownership of a lane.
A session released its claim on a lane.
A lane was added to the catalog.
A lane was removed from the catalog.
Default lane assignments were configured.
A per-lane governance policy was set.
A slice boundary was recorded with its summary, deliverables, and next step.
A message was written to a session inbox.
An action was submitted for operator approval.
A pending approval was approved or denied.
A standing approval policy for a tool was set.
A mailbox message was sent between sessions.
A mailbox message was marked read.
A task was created on the dependency-aware board.
A task's fields or status were updated.
A task was marked complete.
A skill was authored and added to the gallery.
An existing skill was edited.
A skill was removed from the gallery.
A skill body was applied during a session.
A sub-worker subprocess was launched.
A running worker reported it is still alive.
A worker subprocess exited on its own.
A worker subprocess was killed.
A runtime adapter was registered.
A runtime binary was auto-detected on the host.
A runtime adapter was removed.
An MCP server template was registered.
An MCP server was enabled.
An MCP server was disabled.
An MCP server connectivity test ran.
An MCP server registration was removed.
A recurring schedule (cron) was created.
A schedule's definition or enabled state changed.
A schedule was removed.
A schedule reached its trigger time and fired.
A git-backed checkpoint was recorded.
A checkpoint was removed.
A worktree was materialized for a checkpoint.
A rollback to a checkpoint was requested.
An API key was added to the keyring.
An API key was removed from the keyring.
An API key was rotated to a new value.
An API key hit a provider rate limit and was benched.
An external artifact passed the import gate and was accepted.
An external artifact was rejected by the import gate.
A governance proposal was raised for a boss decision.
A governance proposal was decided.
A boss/enforcer message was posted in the governance channel.
The Telegram comms bridge was enabled.
The Telegram comms bridge was disabled.
The Telegram chat allowlist was set.
An inbound Telegram update was received.
An outbound Telegram message was sent.
An outbound Telegram message failed to send.
An inbound Telegram update was dropped (not allowlisted).
The Discord comms bridge was enabled.
The Discord comms bridge was disabled.
The Discord channel allowlist was set.
An outbound Discord message was sent.
An outbound Discord message failed to send.
The email comms bridge was enabled.
The email comms bridge was disabled.
The email (SMTP) configuration was set.
The email recipient allowlist was set.
An email was sent.
An outbound email failed to send.
A follow-up was opened from a slice review finding.
A verification gate ran and recorded its verdict.
A goal with success conditions and constraints was declared.
A declared goal was marked done or abandoned, closing the goal lifecycle.
A queued pending action was drained (executed or resolved).
An action was enqueued for later draining.
A plan phase was entered, optionally raising the governance tier.
A plan phase was explicitly exited.
A slice's functional deliverable was approved.
A slice review completed with a verdict and findings.
A slice locked its intended file scope.
A slice's locked scope was expanded with a reason.
Source-file hashes were recomputed for drift tracking.
A registered trigger fired and dispatched its target.
Agent instruction files were synced.
A stale session was auto-closed by the janitor.
A session was auto-registered by a SessionStart hook.
A session was detected as stale (missed heartbeats).
A pre-compaction checkpoint captured record currency before context compaction.
A fact was imported from a vendor tool's own memory store.
Slash-command definitions were synced to the runtime.
A team was opened with allocated lanes and members.
A lane was allocated to a team.
A member session joined a team.
A member session left a team.
A team was closed.
A multi-stage pipeline run started.
A pipeline run entered a stage.
A pipeline run exited a stage with a status.
A pipeline run completed all stages.
A pipeline run halted before completion.
An advisor (external CLI) was invoked in a subprocess.
An advisor wrote its result artifact.
Token usage for a session/model was reported to the ledger.
Skill bodies were auto-injected into an orientation digest.
A matching skill was withheld from injection for untrusted provenance.
A default framework tool invocation started.
A default framework tool invocation exited.
A tool invocation was refused (allowlist or dangerous form).
The workspace governance tier changed.
A governance behavior-override key was changed or cleared (recorded write-ahead so the discipline off-switch is always on the record).
A persisted plan was created.
A phase was added to a plan.
A plan phase was completed.
A plan phase was blocked with a reason.
A plan was revised (phases added/removed/modified).
A plan was completed.
A plan was cancelled.
A loop (ralph or plan-loop) started.
A loop iteration started.
A loop iteration completed.
A loop halted before its goal.
A loop completed its goal.
A coordinator primitive started driving a plan.
A coordinator entered a plan phase.
A coordinator completed a plan phase.
A coordinator halted.
A coordinator completed the plan.
A lane claim was force-taken from a prior holder.
A candidate skill pattern was detected.
A skill candidate was approved into the gallery.
A skill candidate was rejected.
A supply-chain trust audit ran over dependencies.
A dependency version/hash pin was added.
A dependency pin was removed.
A supply-chain trust violation was detected.
An MCP template's provenance hash verified.
An MCP template's provenance hash did not match.
An MCP server was approved for use.
A worker's environment was filtered against an allowlist.
A secret-shaped value was detected in tool argv.
A skill was imported from an external source.
An imported skill was marked trusted.
A cross-session handoff narrative was set.
Transcripts were mined for failed→succeeded correction pairs.
A no-provider learning digest was written.
A correction candidate was judged by a worker.
A typed correction was written to an agent file or memory.
A memory fact was superseded by a newer fact.
A curated orient/handoff briefing persisted its original for retrieval.
The bridge rejected a request with a non-loopback Host/Origin.
A bridge request selected a workspace other than the active one.
A chain-local tamper-detection cutover anchor (seeded into a freshly-minted sync partition so verify holds it to the post-cutover strict rules).
A mutating tool was let through without a discipline check (enforcement off, a self-disable attempt, or the enforcement hook uninstalled) — a witness so a bypass is never silent.
The self-discipline enforcement path threw and fell open — recorded so a persistent enforcement bug can't hide behind a silent fail-open.

A verification run (goal success-eval, project/self test, or heavy suite) was opened before it ran — a dangling STARTED with no paired RAN is read as non-green, so a crash can't leave a stale pass authoritative.

A verification RECEIPT appended from the runner's in-process result (never a re-read state file) and referencing its paired VERIFICATION_STARTED — the recency/success readouts trust this tamper-detecting spine receipt, not a hand-writable projection.
A spine receipt was stamped into the OpenTimestamps calendars as a new anchor.
An anchor's OpenTimestamps proof file changed (partial merge or completed Bitcoin attestation).
An operator-run consume ceremony recorded an assurance assessment — ledger convenience only, labeled non-authoritative by every consumer.
A blueprint skeleton was distilled into prose by a provider CLI.
The source tree was scanned for deliberate-shortcut debt markers.
The declared architecture contract was checked against the import graph.
A per-turn drift trajectory tag was recorded vs the declared goal.
Sustained un-returned drift raised a swap/revert/continue flag.
Per-lane earned-autonomy trust scores were computed over the record.
An autonomy rung change was recommended (recommend-only).
A worktree checkout was attached to a lane claim.
A removing worktree detach (merged|abandoned) was authorized and begun (durable intent; the attachment stays LIVE until WORKTREE_DETACHED).
A worktree was detached from a lane claim with a disposition.
A dataset-snapshot manifest was validated, hash-pinned, and recorded.
An externally-run training run was recorded as started from its manifest.
A training run's completion (checkpoint + metrics) was recorded.
A model checkpoint was registered under its sha256 identity key.
An externally-run benchmark eval of a checkpoint was recorded.
An eval manifest declared a critical regression (one event per regression).
An operator acknowledged an eval's critical regressions with a recorded reason.
A stage promotion was proposed; its approval request rides the spine first.
A proposed promotion was confirmed against its own allowing approval decision.
A checkpoint approved to released was recorded as released with a rollback plan.
A released checkpoint was rolled back to an earlier stage.
